name: Message Identification
definition: Point-to-point reference assigned by the instructing party to unambiguously identify the message. Unique per message instance.
format: Alphanumeric, no spaces recommended

name: Creation Date Time
definition: Date and time at which the message was created by the instructing party. Must be a valid ISO 8601 datetime.
format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssZ or YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss+hh:mm

name: Number of Transactions
definition: Total number of individual transactions contained in the message. Used for reconciliation and integrity checking.
format: Numeric string (no decimals)

name: Settlement Method
definition: Method used to settle the credit transfer instruction. Determines how interbank settlement occurs.
format: One of: CLRG (Clearing), INDA (Instructed Agent), INGA (Instructing Agent), COVE (Cover)

name: Instruction Identification
definition: Unique identification assigned by the instructing party for the instructed party. Point-to-point reference between adjacent agents.
format: Alphanumeric

name: End-to-End Identification
definition: Unique identification assigned by the initiating party. Carried unchanged through the entire payment chain from originator to beneficiary.
format: Alphanumeric — must be unique per originator

name: Transaction Identification
definition: Unique identification assigned by the first instructing agent. Remains unchanged for the life of the transaction across all agents.
format: Alphanumeric

name: Unique End-to-End Transaction Reference
definition: Universally unique identifier (UUID v4) for tracking a transaction across all systems globally. Introduced by SWIFT gpi.
format: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-4xxx-yxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx (UUID v4)

name: Interbank Settlement Amount
definition: Amount of money to be moved between the instructing agent and the instructed agent at the interbank level. Includes currency attribute.
format: Decimal number with Ccy attribute (ISO 4217 currency code)

name: Instructed Amount
definition: Amount of money to be moved as instructed by the initiating party. Must remain unchanged through the entire payment chain.
format: Decimal number with Ccy attribute (ISO 4217 currency code)

name: Interbank Settlement Date
definition: Date on which the amount of money ceases to be available to the agent that owes it and becomes available to the agent to which it is due.
format: YYYY-MM-DD

name: Charge Bearer
definition: Specifies which party/parties will bear the charges associated with the processing of the payment transaction.
format: One of: DEBT (Debtor), CRED (Creditor), SHAR (Shared), SLEV (Service Level)

name: Debtor
definition: Party that owes an amount of money to the creditor. The debtor's account will be debited. Contains name and postal address sub-elements.
format: Complex type: Nm (Max140Text), PstlAdr (StrtNm, TwnNm, Ctry, PstCd)

name: Debtor Account
definition: Identification of the account of the debtor to which a debit entry will be posted as a result of the transaction.
format: Contains Id with either IBAN or Othr/Id (proprietary account number)

name: Debtor Agent
definition: Financial institution servicing the account of the debtor. Identified by BIC/BICFI code.
format: FinInstnId containing BICFI (8 or 11 character BIC code)

name: Creditor
definition: Party to which an amount of money is due. The creditor's account will be credited. Contains name and postal address sub-elements.
format: Complex type: Nm (Max140Text), PstlAdr (StrtNm, TwnNm, Ctry, PstCd)

name: Creditor Account
definition: Identification of the account of the creditor to which a credit entry will be posted as a result of the transaction.
format: Contains Id with either IBAN or Othr/Id (proprietary account number)

name: Creditor Agent
definition: Financial institution servicing the account of the creditor. Identified by BIC/BICFI code.
format: FinInstnId containing BICFI (8 or 11 character BIC code)

name: International Bank Account Number
definition: Internationally agreed system of identifying bank accounts (ISO 13616). Country code (2) + check digits (2) + BBAN (up to 30).
format: Country code + check digits + BBAN (e.g., DE89370400440532013000)

name: Business Identifier Code (Financial Institution)
definition: Code allocated to a financial institution by the ISO 9362 Registration Authority. Uniquely identifies a bank globally.
format: 8 or 11 alphanumeric characters: BANKCCLL(BBB)

name: Remittance Information
definition: Information supplied to enable matching of payment with items that the transfer is intended to settle, such as invoice numbers.
format: Contains Ustrd (Max140Text unstructured) or Strd (structured with reference details)

name: Transaction Status
definition: Specifies the status of a transaction in the payment lifecycle. Used in status report messages.
format: ACTC (Accepted Technical), ACCP (Accepted Customer Profile), ACSC (Accepted Settlement Completed), RCVD (Received), PDNG (Pending), RJCT (Rejected)

name: Status Reason Information
definition: Detailed information about the reason for the status. Mandatory when TxSts is RJCT. Contains reason code and additional text.
format: Rsn/Cd (4-char reason code) + AddtlInf (Max105Text explanation)

name: Purpose
definition: Underlying reason for the payment transaction. Uses external code sets to classify the payment purpose.
format: Cd: SALA (Salary), SUPP (Supplier Payment), TAXS (Tax), DIVI (Dividend), GIFT (Gift), RENT (Rent)

name: Control Sum
definition: Total of all individual amounts included in the message, irrespective of currencies. Used as an integrity/reconciliation check.
format: Decimal number (e.g., 1850.00)

name: Initiating Party
definition: Party that initiates the payment. This is the entity that creates the pain.001 message and sends it to the debtor's bank.
format: Complex type containing Nm and optional Id (OrgId or PrvtId)

name: Payment Method
definition: Specifies the means of payment that will be used to move the amount of money.
format: TRF (Credit Transfer), CHK (Cheque), DD (Direct Debit)

name: Requested Execution Date
definition: Date at which the initiating party requests the clearing agent to process the payment. Must be a valid business day.
format: YYYY-MM-DD

name: Ultimate Debtor
definition: Ultimate party that owes an amount of money. Used when the actual payer is different from the account holder (debtor).
format: Complex type: Nm (Max140Text)

name: Ultimate Creditor
definition: Ultimate party to which an amount of money is due. Used when the actual beneficiary is different from the account holder (creditor).
format: Complex type: Nm (Max140Text)

name: Acceptance Date Time
definition: Point in time when the payment order from the initiating party meets the processing conditions of the account servicing agent.
format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

name: Payment Information Identification
definition: Unique identification assigned by the sending party to identify the payment information block. Groups related transactions.
format: Alphanumeric
